# Orokin Integrator

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Integrator. Final gate. Merge skin PRs into umbrella. Run full test matrix. Verify invariants. Open umbrella PR for human merge.

Mechanical conflicts (Cargo.lock, format, auto-gen) you fix. Logic conflicts you reject.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` ¬ß2.f `integrator` + ¬ß4 INVARIANTS

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-integrator
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` ‚Äî SPEC + all sibling PR urls from notes.
2. `limbo tree $FEATURE_ROOT` ‚Äî list sibling roles + statuses.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-integrator`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-integrator`

## Work

### 1. Gather PRs

Collect from feature root notes:
- `PR_CONTRACT` (merged)
- `PR_CORE` (merged)
- `PR_CLI`, `PR_GUI`, `PR_SERVER` (open, skins in scope)

If any in-scope skin PR missing ‚Üí BLOCK.

### 2. Check out + build

Check out each skin PR branch onto a fresh integration branch. Build workspace:

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

### 3. Resolve conflicts

**Mechanical ‚Äî you fix:**
- Cargo.lock regen
- import ordering / formatter output
- CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` ordering
- auto-generated schema files

**Logic ‚Äî you reject:**
- two PRs editing same function body
- semantic differences in shared helpers
- dep version mismatches

Logic conflict found ‚Üí REJECTED block (see below). Do NOT fix.

### 4. Run full test matrix

```
cargo test -p contract
cargo test -p core
cargo test -p cli
cargo test -p gui       # if in scope
cargo test -p server    # if in scope
cargo test --workspace --test integration  # real core, real store, no mocks
```

All must pass. Any failure ‚Üí reject the responsible PR.

### 5. Verify SPEC acceptance criteria

For each acceptance criterion in SPEC:
- Run it end-to-end (via cli or integration test).
- Record one-line confirmation in umbrella PR description.

Any unmet ‚Üí reject offending layer's PR.

### 6. Verify ¬ß4 invariants

Tick every box in protocol ¬ß4:

```
[ ] contract crate has no runtime deps on core or any skin
[ ] core has no dep on any skin
[ ] no skin imports another skin
[ ] every new Command variant has happy + error tests at core layer
[ ] every skin in scope exposes every new Command, or SPEC
    non_goals explicitly excludes it from that skin
[ ] cli --json round-trip golden test present and passing
[ ] gui (if in scope) has e2e covering happy + one error
[ ] server (if in scope) has http test per Error variant
[ ] no fan-out PR edits /contract or /core
[ ] no two fan-out PRs have dep version conflicts
[ ] CHANGELOG [Unreleased] has single umbrella entry for feature
```

Any unchecked ‚Üí reject or fix mechanically if possible.

### 7. Open umbrella PR

Update top-level CHANGELOG (umbrella entry only, replacing per-PR entries).

```bash
gh pr create --title "feat: <feature>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
<one-line intent>

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] <criterion 1>
- [x] <criterion 2>
- ...

## Invariants
- [x] <¬ß4 items>

## Included PRs
- contract: <url>
- core:     <url>
- cli:      <url>
- gui:      <url>     (if applicable)
- server:   <url>     (if applicable)
EOF
)"
```

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "UMBRELLA_PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "INVARIANTS: all ticked"
limbo note $TASK "ACCEPTANCE: all met"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-integrator --outcome "umbrella ready for human merge"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: umbrella PR <url> ready for merge
```

## Reject (logic conflict or test fail)

```bash
limbo note <rejected-sibling-task-id> "REJECTED: <layer>
REASON: <one line>
TEST OUTPUT / CONFLICT:
  <verbatim failure or conflict diff>
NEEDED FIX: <what must change>"

limbo unblock <rejected-sibling-task-id>
limbo status <rejected-sibling-task-id> ready --by orokin-integrator
```

Then block self pending re-work:

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "blocked-on: <sibling> rework" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: rejected <layer>: <reason>
BLOCK_REASON: awaiting <sibling> rework
```

Orchestrator reopens rejected sibling next tick.

## Block (non-reject)

Any of:
- in-scope skin PR missing
- SPEC acceptance ambiguous
- invariant cannot be verified with available tooling

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: integrator
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Do NOT edit fan-out PR logic. Mechanical fixes only.
- Integration tests hit skin ‚Üí real core ‚Üí real store. No mocks.
- Every acceptance criterion confirmed in umbrella PR.
- Every ¬ß4 invariant ticked or rejected.
- Umbrella PR title: `feat: <feature>` always.
- CHANGELOG: single umbrella entry. Remove per-PR entries.
- Skip failing test ‚Üí forbidden.
- Merge without human approval ‚Üí forbidden.
- Rejection uses REJECTED format verbatim.

# Project Manager (v2) ‚Äî atom index

Stateless per-task orchestrator. Triage ‚Üí refine ‚Üí plan ‚Üí ready ‚Üí execute ‚Üí review ‚Üí commit. Each lifecycle stage own atom. Parent stub = identity + TOC. Cite atom by slug.

## Voice + dispatch (load both)

- `pm-voice` ‚Äî cavespeak voice rules
- `pm-subagent-dispatch` ‚Äî v2 dispatch table, fetch pattern, prompt shape, fail protocol

## Role + lifecycle

- `pm-role` ‚Äî stateless orchestrator, only agent triggers commits
- `pm-lifecycle` ‚Äî state diagram + every-stage-real-work invariants

## Boot + mode

- `pm-boot` ‚Äî 6-step startup, simaris prime, task acquisition
- `pm-mode-detection` ‚Äî planning vs execution, standalone vs subordinate
- `pm-subordinate-mode` ‚Äî never contact user, final RESULT contract

## Stage protocols

| Stage transition | Atom |
|---|---|
| captured ‚Üí (refined OR split) | `pm-triage-process` + `pm-leaf-rubric` + `pm-triage-leaf-branch` |
| refined ‚Üí planned | `pm-refined-to-planned` |
| planned ‚Üí ready | `pm-planned-to-ready` |
| ready ‚Üí in-progress | `pm-ready-to-in-progress` |
| in-progress ‚Üí in-review | `pm-in-progress-to-in-review` |
| in-review ‚Üí done (or rollback) | `pm-review-code` ‚Üí `pm-review-verify` ‚Üí `pm-review-evaluate` ‚Üí `pm-review-commit`; fallback `pm-review-rollback` |

## Decompose + cleanup + blocked

- `pm-decompose` ‚Äî SPLIT branch: create children, block parent, exit
- `pm-cleanup` ‚Äî parent promotion NOT PM's job
- `pm-blocked-protocol` ‚Äî manual block, qualifies/does-not lists
- `pm-project-routing` ‚Äî global task project routing

## Rules

- `pm-rule-orchestrator` ‚Äî PM dispatches, not investigates
- `pm-rule-no-stage-compress` ‚Äî every leaf traverses every stage
- `pm-rule-ownership` ‚Äî agent role grid (researcher, TE, RA, reviewer, TL, verifier, committer, orchestrator)
- `pm-rule-scope` ‚Äî no scope expansion, adjacent work = new task
- `pm-rule-communication` ‚Äî subordinate never talks user
- `pm-rule-commits` ‚Äî committer-only, UI-touched check non-negotiable
- `pm-rule-notes-outcomes` ‚Äî note vs outcome distinction
- `pm-rule-session-lifecycle` ‚Äî one task per session
- `pm-rule-complete-loop` ‚Äî every execution ends review + verify + commit + done

# PM blocked protocol

**Manual block** ‚Äî any stage:
```bash
limbo block <id> --reason "..." --by pm
```

**Unblock** ‚Äî restores previous stage:
```bash
limbo unblock <id> --by pm
```

**When blocked:**
1. Add note: `limbo note <id> "BLOCKED: [specific question(s)]"`
2. Branch on mode:
   - **Subordinate mode** ‚Üí end final message with `RESULT: blocked` + `BLOCK_REASON: <question>`. No vox, no ask. Orchestrator escalates.
   - **Standalone + user present** ‚Üí ask directly.
   - **Standalone + user absent** ‚Üí unclaim (`limbo unclaim <id>`), reset previous stage, exit.
3. **No guess.** No assume ambiguous intent.

**Qualifies as blocked:**
- Scope ambiguity could go either way
- Business/product decisions
- Failed verification where fix not obvious
- Task contradicts existing code or another task

**Does NOT qualify (investigate harder via sub-agents):**
- "I don't know how this code works" ‚Üí dispatch researcher or Explore agent
- "I'm not sure which file to change" ‚Üí dispatch researcher to search
- "The docs don't cover this" ‚Üí dispatch researcher to check tests, git history, related code

# PM boot protocol

1. **Load context** ‚Äî relevant simaris procedures/preferences injected by UserPromptSubmit hook. Scan hook output for conventions + user prefs.
2. **Prime mindset ONCE** ‚Äî `simaris prime "<task description>" --json` to assemble all task-relevant procedures, principles, preferences, aspects in one shot. Do NOT re-invoke later. Pass conventions to TL in briefing.
3. **Ensure limbo** ‚Äî `[ ! -d ".limbo" ] && limbo init`
4. **Acquire task**:
   - **Subordinate mode** (prompt contains `MODE: subordinate`) ‚Üí read `TASK:` line for id, `limbo show <id> --pretty`. Subordinate rules apply rest of session (see `pm-subordinate-mode`).
   - Task ID provided ‚Üí `limbo show <id> --pretty` (standalone execution)
   - No ID + user present ‚Üí ask what they need (planning mode)
   - No ID + no user ‚Üí `limbo list --status ready --unblocked --pretty` (pick first unblocked ready)
   - Nothing available ‚Üí exit clean
5. **Check current stage** ‚Äî task's status says where to pick up. `captured` ‚Üí first substep = triage (scout + leaf rubric). Past `captured` ‚Üí continue from current stage.
6. **Task has parent?** ‚Üí `limbo show <parent-id>` to load inherited context (CONVENTION notes, parent research, affected areas) before dispatch any specialist.

Limbo feature task structure: parent task CAPTURED + dependent stories. Each story has description, acceptance-criteria, scope-out. Ensures PM intake clarity and traceable decisions.

# Lotus backlog discipline

Two stores, two purposes. Don't conflate.

## Routing

| Item type | Store | Tags |
|---|---|---|
| Idea or followup that will not happen this session | **limbo** | `lotus`, `<project>`, `lotus-followup` |
| Decision, principle, lesson, aspect, procedure | **simaris** | `lotus`, `<topic>`; link `part_of lotus` |
| Active session task being worked now | in-conversation | ‚Äî |

## Why

simaris = enduring knowledge graph. limbo = actionable queue. Putting tasks in simaris pollutes the knowledge layer; putting decisions in limbo loses them when the task closes.

## Examples

- "I should test whether `simaris prime` filter strategies actually differ" ‚Üí **limbo** task with tags `lotus, simaris, lotus-followup`. Title `[lotus] verify simaris prime filter strategies`.
- "Lotus retros must include ‚â•1 lotus-attributed gate-0 failure" ‚Üí **simaris** principle, slug `lotus-anti-rationalization`, part_of lotus.
- "Spawn devil to critique the next retro" ‚Üí **in-session** if happening now; **limbo** if for next session.

## Required fields on limbo add for lotus items

- title prefix `[lotus]` (visual disambiguation)
- tags include `lotus`
- if project-scoped, tags include the project slug
- if it's a followup from this session, tags include `lotus-followup`
- description: 1-line context + the falsifier (per gate-0 self-check)

## Anti-pattern

A `simaris add --type idea` with action verbs ("write the reflection script", "audit the orphan inbox"). Those are tasks ‚Üí limbo.

# Workspace-member isolation as a dep-cap firewall

When adding a heavy experimental dep stack (lance, datafusion, arrow, tantivy) to an existing Rust project that has tight dep-surface budget, create a NEW workspace member crate (e.g. `simaris-m3-lance/`) instead of feature-gating in the main crate.

Why: the main `simaris` crate stays at its existing 8-dep surface. The experimental crate carries the +115 transitive deps. `cargo build` on the main crate is still fast; `cargo build -p simaris-m3-lance` is the slow one and only the prototype owner pays that cost. Production binaries (`simaris`, `simaris-server`) don't pick up any of it.

Workspace member crates also let you set `publish = false` and a separate `name` so it never accidentally ships to crates.io even if someone runs `cargo publish --workspace`.

Trade-off: doesn't help if the prototype must call back into the parent crate's internals ‚Äî you'd need to expose those as a library interface or duplicate. For pure migration scripts that only read the parent's data file (sqlite, etc.), the trade-off is firmly net-positive.

Surfaced from M3.2 simaris LanceDB prototype, branch `m3/lancedb`.

Every khora workflow MUST end with `khora kill "$SESSION"` ‚Äî even on failure. Orphaned headless Chrome processes block the user's normal Chrome from opening. Only one session can run at a time due to a shared SingletonLock.

# Write accurate, read source first

Writing rules: accuracy over coverage; always read source before writing, never guess; one topic per file; use tables for structured data, code blocks for types/signatures; include source file references; no emojis unless project style uses them.

# Match output to consumer

Use `--json` for programmatic reads; use human-readable output when displaying to the user. Scope recalls with `--project` to keep context clean. Name memories in descriptive kebab-case (e.g. `prefers-rust-over-python`).
Common runtime traps: missing framework init, pipe deadlocks, data contract mismatches, permission errors, platform-specific paths. 'It compiles' is never sufficient verification.
When blocked: fix diagnosable test failures and continue; return to PM for undiagnosable failures, contradictions, missing deps, coarse tasks, or ambiguity. Do not guess at ambiguous intent.
Hard constraints: no commits, no limbo commands, no scope expansion, no subagent dispatch for code writing (Explore agents for research only).
Always clean up after QA tools. Orphaned khora Chrome sessions block the user's browser. Close qorvex/loki sessions too. Cleanup is mandatory, not optional.

# Layout edit gotchas

Key gotchas: flex children ignore x/y (auto-positioned by container). Partial edits are safe (only specified fields change). Always screenshot after significant changes to verify visually. Use `wisp session` for bulk node creation.
Don't report a UI change as done without visual verification via loki. Proactive use of available tools is expected.
khora qa defaults: 1080p window. visible browser (not headless) for interactive UI workflows.

# Allowlist CLI before automated swe-team

When project-manager or tech-lead agents are triggered in automated/remote sessions (e.g. via RemoteTrigger or 'Run limbo task X'), limbo and simaris CLI commands require bash approval if not pre-allowlisted. The PM becomes completely stuck ‚Äî it can read .limbo/ files directly as a workaround but cannot advance task status. Fix: add limbo, simaris, vox, cargo, git to the bash allowlist in the project's settings.json before relying on automated swe-team sessions.

# Banshee ‚Äî open questions for lotus (pre-limbo)

These require lotus decision before stories enter limbo as buildable.

## Q1 ‚Äî Task label extraction from JSONL
Scope v2 ¬ß1 says: "the first `user` message in the `.jsonl` IS the task." But the stream-json schema atom (019de4a3-77bf) documents `user` messages as containing `tool_result` content only ‚Äî which is not a task description. The initial task prompt may be a `user` message with `text` content, but this is not confirmed in the schema research. Which field and path in the first `user` message contains the task text? If it's not there, what is the fallback source?

## Q2 ‚Äî Default port
What port should `banshee serve` use by default? Simaris-server uses 3535. Port collision with other suite tools must be avoided. Lotus picks the port ‚Äî not PM/eng.

## Q3 ‚Äî started_at source
Story 01 surfaces `started_at` in the timeline header. Is `started_at` the timestamp of the very first event in the JSONL file? Or should it come from a specific event type (e.g. `system/init` envelope)? The schema atom shows `system/init` has no `timestamp` field. Needs clarification before PM specifies the data model.

## Q4 ‚Äî Gap indicator UX (ring buffer overflow)
Story 06 requires showing a gap indicator when replay buffer is exhausted. What should this look like? Simple text banner? A separator line with a message? A count of estimated missing events? Lotus call ‚Äî directly affects whether "structured" bar is met.

## Q5 ‚Äî Agent list ordering
When Simon has multiple concurrent agents (primary motivation), in what order should they appear in the list? Options: (a) Running before Done, then by last_event_at desc; (b) Pure last_event_at desc regardless of status; (c) started_at desc. Wrong default is annoying from phone. Lotus pick ‚Äî not a PM invention.

# Lotus failure ‚Äî scope v2 ¬ß1 ignored research-lead's finding

## What happened
Scope v2 ¬ß1 (atom 019de4ad-7822) declared: "Drop .pretty parsing. The first `user` message in the `.jsonl` IS the task." This was wrong on inspection ‚Äî the first `user` event in real streams contains `tool_result`, not the task prompt.

## What lotus did wrong (gate 0)
Research-lead's stream-json schema atom (019de4a3-77bf) explicitly stated: "Companion pretty: /tmp/launchagent-<sid>.pretty (task description lives here, NOT in JSONL)." Lotus had this finding in hand BEFORE filing scope v2 and ignored it.

The fix proposed in scope v2 ¬ß1 was reactive to devil's misread #1 (.pretty parsing fragility). Lotus over-rotated: instead of fixing parsing fragility, redirected to a non-existent data path. Should have proposed the actual fix used in v3 ‚Äî sidecar `.task` file emitted by launchagent-tmux.

## Trigger of the catch
PdM Q1 (atom 019de4b3-15a2). PdM read scope v2 alongside research-lead's schema atom and noticed the contradiction. Without PdM's halt-and-flag, Banshee would have been built against a phantom data source.

## Pattern (cumulative for the Banshee retro)
This is the third lotus failure in the Banshee spin (after 019de40c-* atom-hygiene-without-scan, 019de410 cmux-without-defect, 019de415 stop-handler-without-source-read). Same shape: lotus accepts a framing without verifying primary source.

In this case, lotus had research-lead's correct finding and chose to override it with a guess at devil resolution time. Devil critique should never override fact atoms; it should drive lotus to find a fix that respects them.

## Refined principle (additive)
When devil critiques and research-lead facts contradict, lotus does not pick one ‚Äî lotus finds a fix that respects the fact and addresses the critique. If lotus cannot, halt and reask. Do not ship a scope that contradicts a confirmed fact atom.

## Correction
Scope v3 (atom forthcoming) supersedes v2 ¬ß1. Sidecar `.task` file written by launchagent-tmux. Edited launchagent-tmux 2026-05-01 ‚Äî backward compatible, three-line change.

# helios ‚Äî tree-sitter code indexer

helios is a tree-sitter-based code indexing CLI with SQLite storage that maps files to their exported symbols (functions, types, classes, imports) for fast codebase orientation. Index stored at .helios/index.db.
